mnemonics: Dense Like Crowds: A set is dense if you can always find one of its points (a person) arbitrarily close to any point in the space (like always being near someone in a dense crowd).
keyPoints: A subset D of a metric space (X,d) is dense if its closure is the entire space: Cl(D) = X.
A metric space is separable if it contains a countable, dense subset.
The rational numbers Q are a countable dense subset of the real numbers R, making R a separable space.
commonMistakes: Confusing a dense set with an open set. A dense set does not need to be open (e.g., Q is dense in R but not open).
Assuming a non-separable space cannot have any dense subsets. It can have uncountable dense subsets, but separability requires one that is countable.
